coverage: Cross-backend generated output and runtime behavior is covered by Rust C, WASM, and LLVM oracle tests.
C/header snapshots, checked C behavior, clang build flow, and public C package APIs are covered by Rust C backend and CLI tests.
Type checker success, diagnostics, helper lookup APIs, f64 checks, and package root check API parity are covered.
Lexer, parser, checker, and CLI diagnostic formatting are covered against TypeScript-compatible output.
CLI help, check, emit, build, diagnostics, output errors, and backend runtime flows are covered by Rust CLI and backend oracle tests.
Token kinds, text, spans, diagnostics, UTF-16 offsets, and package root lex parity are covered.
LLVM object and dynamic-library runtime behavior for bool-oriented official examples is covered.
LLVM object and dynamic-library runtime behavior for nested calls is covered.
LLVM object and dynamic-library runtime behavior for if/else and while control flow is covered.
LLVM object and dynamic-library runtime behavior for pointer, index, field, and f64 memory access is covered.
LLVM object and dynamic-library runtime behavior for pricing fixtures is covered.
LLVM scalar object output, dynamic-library behavior, target detection, and build-llvm CLI behavior are covered.
LLVM object and dynamic-library runtime behavior for short-circuit control flow is covered.
LLVM generated IR for call-heavy official examples is covered against the TypeScript oracle.
LLVM generated IR for control-flow examples is covered against the TypeScript oracle.
LLVM generated IR for memory, pointer, field, and f64 memory examples is covered.
LLVM generated IR and runtime behavior for pricing fixtures is covered.
LLVM scalar, f64, explicit cast, and no-fast-math generated IR behavior is covered.
LLVM short-circuit RHS block generation and runtime behavior is covered.
LLVM module headers, target handling, struct declarations, and function skeletons are covered through generated IR oracle tests.
LLVM pointer, index, field, and memory GEP behavior is covered through memory generated IR and runtime tests.
Stable LLVM textual output is covered through whole-IR oracle comparisons.
LLVM struct layout and f64 storage mappings are covered through generated IR and runtime memory tests.
LLVM naming is covered through stable generated IR comparisons for official examples.
LLVM scalar, pointer, struct, signedness, and unresolved type mappings are covered through generated IR and runtime tests.
C emission from MIR control-flow blocks is covered by C backend oracle and MIR lowering tests.
C emission from scalar, call, f64, and cast MIR is covered by C backend oracle tests.
CFG simplification, constant branches, jump chains, while CFG validity, and short-circuit preservation are covered.
Integer and f64 local CSE, address CSE, store/call invalidation, and C output reuse are covered.
Small helper inlining, f64-safe inlining, exported function retention, recursion rejection, and threshold behavior are covered.
Natural loop analysis, LICM, induction handling, f64 restrictions, checked-mode restrictions, and C checked-loop behavior are covered.
MIR lowering for calls, nested calls, argument expressions, and explicit int-to-f64 builtins is covered.
MIR lowering for if/else, if without else, while loops, and returns in loop bodies is covered.
MIR place lowering for index, field, f64 pointer, and store/load behavior is covered across MIR and backend tests.
MIR lowering for scalar straight-line, f64 literals, arithmetic, unary minus, and comparisons is covered.
MIR and backend lowering for short-circuit logical operators is covered.
Constant folding, copy propagation, DCE, opt-level behavior, f64 sensitivity, and optimized MIR parity are covered.
Optimization pipeline names, pass order, validation, O0 behavior, pass debug printing, and changed-state behavior are covered.
Stable MIR text printing for structs, functions, f64 constants, casts, and CLI emit-mir output is covered.
MIR validation is exercised by lowering, optimization, and backend oracle tests that reject invalid type and control-flow states.
WAT emission and runtime behavior for internal and exported calls is covered.
WAT emission and runtime behavior for basic blocks, jumps, branches, and returns is covered.
WAT emission and runtime behavior for pointer, index, field, and f64 memory operations is covered.
WAT emission and runtime behavior for pricing fixtures is covered.
WAT emission and runtime behavior for scalar, f64, comparison, and explicit cast code is covered.
WAT short-circuit control-flow emission and runtime behavior is covered.
Canonical calckernel package metadata, ckc command name, legacy .ik rejection, and replacement docs are covered.
Fresh npm install, ckc bin mapping, wrapper dispatch, and package root API smoke behavior are covered.
Parser AST, precedence, diagnostics, package root parse parity, and CLI parser diagnostic behavior are covered.
Integer, bool, f64, literal, assignment, and materialization behavior is covered through checker and package API tests.
CKWasmArena construction, error boundaries, allocation, view refresh, copy helpers, and f64 interop are covered.
WASM runtime behavior for calls is covered through official example interop tests.
WASM runtime behavior for control flow is covered through official example interop tests.
WASM runtime behavior for memory, f64 pointers, casts, and official f64 memory examples is covered.
WASM runtime behavior for pricing fixtures is covered.
WASM runtime behavior for scalar, f64 scalar, and explicit cast functions is covered.
WASM runtime behavior for short-circuit paths is covered.
WASM ABI type, pointer, primitive, struct padding, nested struct, and f64 layout behavior is covered by WAT and runtime tests.
Official WASM f64-sum, f64-axpy, and pricing-SoA examples are covered by backend and package helper tests.
WAT module shape, function skeletons, locals, escaping, and stable output are covered by WAT oracle tests.
